subject: grammar
title: Verb Patterns: Infinitive or -ing form
instruction: Fill in the gaps with the correct form of the verbs in brackets.
passage:
Tutor: So have you managed 1 [1] (finish) the assignment, Kumiko?
Kumiko: Well, I've nearly finished but I forgot 2 [2] (add) a bibliography, so I'd like 3 [3] (have) an extension, if that's possible?
Tutor: How long will you need 4 [4] (do) it?
Kumiko: I aim 5 [5] (finish) it today, but maybe two days would be the best thing.
Tutor: Yes, that's fine. Have you begun 6 [6] (work) on your dissertation yet?
Kumiko: Yes, I started 7 [7] (write) my questionnaire a couple of weeks ago, but I stopped 8 [8] (complete) this assignment. I'm planning 9 [9] (get) the questionnaire ready by the end of the week, and I'm hoping 10 [10] (show) it to you for your comments before I use it. Could we meet next week?
Tutor: Yes, of course. I'm afraid I can't find my diary though. I remember 11 [11] (put) it in my briefcase this morning, but it doesn't seem 12 [12] (be) there. Why don't you email me with a suitable time?
Kumiko: Yes, okay.
Tutor: So, I'll see you in the seminar tomorrow. Don't forget 13 [13] (email) me!
Kumiko: Thank you, I'll try 14 [14] (remember).
